finalized_plan.description: Open the NeurIPS 2022 submissions page on OpenReview and scan across all pages to identify papers that have at least one author with the name “Yuri” by using a page search for “Yuri.”
Create a list of candidate papers by collecting URLs of those submissions that list at least one “Yuri” in the author fields
For each candidate paper, visit its detail page to locate the review metadata section
Within each detail page, search for the review recommendation metadata to check if the value “certain” is present
Tally the papers that meet both criteria: (a) they list an author named “Yuri” and (b) they have a review recommendation of “certain.”
Compile the final verified numeric count along with the methodology, including specific links (e.g., the conference page) and details on how the filtering was performed
Provide the answer with a summary overview, a detailed explanation, and additional context as required

root_cause_reasoning: The agent initially attempted to search for relevant pages on OpenReview, but after repeatedly failing to scroll down, it switched to using Google Search to retrieve relevant pages. It also found some additional papers in the process, but output the final result without visiting them.
